summary: Backend-only verification of iteration 168 changes: (1) NEW DELETE /api/expenses/{id} with shift total_expenses recompute, (2) business_date injection in customer_menu/sync_routes/customer-sandbox order creation paths, (3) auto_migrate_business_dates v2 corrective pass with system_flags guard, (4) POST /api/admin/migrate-business-dates?force=true, (5) GET /api/reports/expenses by_cashier.items 'id' field. Created /app/backend/tests/test_expense_delete_iter168.py with 9 pytest cases. Result: 8 PASSED, 1 SKIPPED (no menu items configured for POS order injection test). All NEW DELETE endpoint behaviours verified end-to-end: (a) 404 for nonexistent expense, (b) 401 for unauthenticated, (c) creates expense with business_date inherited from active shift, (d) DELETE returns {success:true, message:...} and re-computes shift total_expenses excluding refund category. Migration force=true endpoint accepts the param, returns 200 with stats dict; second consecutive force run returns orders_updated=0 / expenses_updated=0 (proving stats report ACTUAL changes only, not just rows scanned). system_flags collection contains {flag: 'orders_business_date_v2_fixed', applied_at: ...} confirming startup v2 migration ran exactly once. Non-force migration reports 0 updates (idempotent). by_cashier.items in /api/reports/expenses now includes 'id' field for every item.

minor.issue: No singular GET endpoint for a shift by id exists (404). Tests must use /api/shifts/current or /api/shifts list. Not introduced by iter168, but worth adding for clearer client integration.

When a NEW expense is POSTed during an open shift, shift.total_expenses is NOT incrementally updated in DB at create time (verified: live total = before_total even after expense creation). The DELETE /api/expenses/{id} correctly re-computes total_expenses from scratch (subtracts the deleted amount and any other refund-excluded sums). Recommend mirroring the same recompute logic in POST /api/expenses for consistency, OR document that total_expenses is only recomputed at shift close + on expense delete.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Mirror DELETE's shift total_expenses recompute logic into POST /api/expenses so the live shift total updates immediately when an expense is added (currently only updated on shift close or on expense delete).
OPTIONAL: Add GET /api/shifts/{shift_id} singular endpoint for client convenience.

Cashier-role 403 test could not be executed programmatically (no cashier in the admin tenant has a known password). Verified by code review only: server.py:3097 requires role in ['admin','super_admin','manager','branch_manager'] otherwise raises 403 with message 'غير مصرح - المالك أو المدير فقط'.
critical_code_review_comments: DELETE /api/expenses/{id} (server.py 3094-3142): Role guard correct (admin/super_admin/manager/branch_manager only). Tenant isolation correct (uses get_user_tenant_id with $or fallback for legacy nulls). 404 returned for non-existent. Shift recompute loop correctly: (a) finds candidate shifts on same branch with started_at <= expense.created_at, (b) filters out shifts that already closed before the expense, (c) excludes category='refund' from the resum, (d) recomputes expected_cash = opening_cash + cash_sales - total_exp. CORRECT.

auto_migrate_business_dates v2 (server.py 513-537): Uses db.system_flags.find_one({flag:'orders_business_date_v2_fixed'}) as guard - verified flag IS inserted after first run with applied_at timestamp. Subsequent restarts will skip the corrective pass. CORRECT.

POST /api/admin/migrate-business-dates?force=true (server.py 19282-19400+): force param added; when true, fetches ALL orders/expenses regardless of business_date existence and only increments stats[xxx]_updated when the new value DIFFERS from existing (line 19362, 19379). Verified: second consecutive force run returns 0 changes - stats report ACTUAL changes only. CORRECT.
routes/customer_menu.py:330,354 - customer order POST inserts business_date via resolve_business_date(tenant_id, order.branch_id). CORRECT.
routes/sync_routes.py:165 - offline-sync order POST inserts business_date via resolve_business_date. CORRECT.
server.py:17304 - admin sandbox customer order POST also inserts business_date via _resolve_business_date. CORRECT.
routes/reports_routes.py:388-394 - by_cashier.items now includes 'id' field. Verified via /api/reports/expenses response inspection. CORRECT.

MINOR: POST /api/expenses (server.py around line 2962) creates expense with business_date but does NOT recompute live shift.total_expenses. Discrepancy: DELETE recomputes; POST does not. End-user could see total_expenses=0 even after adding 100k. Not a regression, but inconsistent with DELETE behaviour.

Inherited issue (still present): OrderResponse / second ShiftResponse in server.py do not declare 'business_date' field (model_config extra='ignore' strips it from API). Already flagged in iter166 - DB has the field correctly.
Iteration 168 changes are minimal, focused and correct. No new bugs introduced. The 75K/339K orphan-expense scenario is fully covered by the new DELETE endpoint + auto-recompute logic.

success_rate.backend: 100% of executed tests (8 passed / 8 executed; 1 skipped due to no menu items in test tenant)

seed_data_creation: Three TEST_-prefixed expenses were created during testing and ALL were deleted by the same tests (DELETE /api/expenses/{id}). No leftover test data. No real tenant expenses were touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter168 features verified working. New DELETE /api/expenses/{id} endpoint correctly recomputes shift.total_expenses on delete (excludes refund category, recomputes expected_cash). Migration force=true correctly reports actual-changes-only. system_flags 'orders_business_date_v2_fixed' is set after first startup; subsequent migrations are no-ops. The cashier-role 403 path could not be tested programmatically (no cashier user has a known password in the test tenant) - verified by code review only at server.py:3097. One non-blocking inconsistency: POST /api/expenses does NOT live-update shift.total_expenses (only DELETE does). This wasn't part of the review_request scope but should be noted. test_pos_order_has_business_date is currently skipped because no menu items exist with available=true and price>0 in the test tenant; if menu items are added, this test will validate that POS-created orders show up in today's sales report (proving business_date was applied).

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: User reported: (1) orphan 75K maintenance expense appearing on day 21 with no delete capability, (2) post-midnight orders during day-21 shift showing under day 22, (3) closing report showing inflated 414K instead of correct 339K.
rca_of_the_issue.fix_summary: Iter168 adds: (a) DELETE /api/expenses/{id} owner/manager-only endpoint with automatic shift total_expenses recompute (excluding refund category); (b) business_date injection in 3 more order creation paths (customer_menu, sync_routes, customer-sandbox) so all orders inherit the active shift's business_date; (c) auto-migration v2 corrective pass with system_flags guard to fix legacy orders that had wrong business_date from earlier migrations; (d) force=true on manual migration to re-run with corrected logic.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: 8/8 executed tests PASS. DELETE returns 404/200 correctly, recomputes shift totals correctly. Migration force=true reports actual changes (0 on second run). system_flags v2 flag is set in DB. by_cashier.items in expense report now includes 'id' for delete-button frontend wiring.
